def: s.f. A parrot, paroquet; — the small singing bird Loxia rosea, or Carpodacus erythrinus; — (met.) a sweet-tongued or eloquent speaker

A common winter visitant throughout India, often caged. 2. ( Poet .) An eloquent speaker. tūtī bulvā' nā , v. a. To acquire fame; get a name. Bole jo sūm bhaṛvā mār uske sar pe jūtī, Do din to dostoṅ meṅ bulvā le apnī tūtī! Nazīr. If speaks the miser-pimp a word shoe-beat him on the head, For two days e'en amongst your friends a good name do but spread! tūtī bolnā , v. n. To have the chief's ear; to acquire influence or authority. Āj kal Sirishtedār sāhab kā khūb tūtī boltā hai. tūtī kī āvāz . The notes of the tūtī. Naqqār-khāne meṅ tūtī kī āvāz kaun suntā hai? Prov. Who would listen in a concert to the poor notes of the parrot?

To rub or twist oneʼs parrot-like lips, is used by way of an affectionate or friendly admonition; thus: بولا نکراي يار تو هر يک سي بگڙکر مل ڐاليگا طوطي سي کوٴي هونٿهـ پکڙکر Bolā na kar ai yār tū har yak se bigaṛkar; mal ḍālegā t̤ūt̤ī se koʼī honṭh pakaṛkar, Do not, my love, indulge in the habit of speaking angrily to every one; lest some one should twist those lips of thine which emulate the parrotʼs in redness.

def: v.n. To go upwards, to rise, ascend, mount (to); to swell, flow over; to boil, boil over, bubble up; to swell with pride or rage, to be puffed up, be inflamed; to abound; to be flushed (with); to overflow, be flooded, be inundated. — ubal ćalnā, v.n. more: v. n. 1. To boil; bubble; boil up. Haṅdyā ubal chalī . 2. To abound; flow over; to be flushed with. Uske hāṅ to paisā ubal paṛā . 3. To overflow; to be flooded, inundated. Naddī ubal chalī . 4. To swell (sūjnā) .; to be inflamed. Garmī se āṅkheṅ ubalyāīṅ . Hin. 5. To be puffed up; to swell with pride. Ser kī hāṅḍī meṅ savā ser paṛā, aur ublā . Prov. 6. To grow brighter (complexion); to become more fair and beautiful. Saut kā raṅg to din-ba-din ublā ātā hai . Wom. ubal chalnā , v. n. 1. To boil over; run over; effervesce. 2. To begin to swell or fill out. Bhāt ubal chalā . The rice is filling out. 3. To be in high spirits; to be flushed, exhilarated, etc. Kyā hī, Nazīr, rāt ko aish ke the muqāble! Maë ke nashe ubal chale, dil ke farākh hausle . Nazīr. What pleasure exquisite was yours, Nazīr, last night; Th' intoxicating wine, th'expanded heart's delight. chhā'tī ubal'nā , v. n. To melt; …

def: v. n. See ابلنا ubalnā.
v. a. (dakh. caus. of اوبلنا) To boil, to make boil: see ابالنا ubālnā.
v.t. To boil, to make boil.
more: v. a. To boil; to cause to boil; to boil over. Chāṅval ubāl lo. ubālī, ubālā, ubālā subālā , adj. 1. Boiled, not seasoned with ghī and spices. 2. Unseasoned, tasteless (dish) ( ogrā ). Kyā, ubālī dāl khilāoge?
(caus. of ابلنا q. v.) v. a. To boil, to make boil.
wety: Urdu verb set ابلنا ابالنا ابلانا ابلوانا Inherited from Sanskrit *उद्वालयति (*udvālayati) (compare वालयति (vālayati)), Proto-Indo-European *wel-. Compare Punjabi ਉਬਾਲਣਾ (ubālṇā), Marathi उबाळा (ubāḷā).

def: v.t. To sow, plant; to cause to germinate, cause to grow; to fructify
more: v. a. Caus. of ubnā S. उत्पद्, H. upānā generate. 1. To sow; plant ( bonā ). 2. To cause to grow; to fructify; to give the increase. Bhāī, bīj bakher to dȳo hai, Bhagwān ubā de, ubā de! Rus.
v. a. To sow, to plant.

def: s.m. Release, deliverance.
Mag. adj. Uneven ( ūṅchā nīchā ); rough; rugged; bad. Gāṛī sambhāl-kar hāṅ ko, dhartī ūbaṛ khābaṛ hai .
adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)

ety: ubār˚ = Prk. उव्वारे(इ) = S. उद्वारय(ति), caus. of rt. उद्+वृ
def: v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.
more: v. a. To set at liberty, liberate, release.
v. a. (dakh. caus. of ابرنا ubarnā ) To reserve, to keep in.

def: v.n. To be left over as a remainder or surplus, to remain over, be over; to exceed; to be kept in reserve, be put by. — ubrā-subrā, s.m. Remainder; surplus; remains, fragments, remnants; leavings; scraps; refuse.
more: E. v. n. To exceed; to be over; to remain over and above. Phr. To be enough and to spare. Dhān koṭhī se ubrā . E.
v. n. (dakh.) To remain over and above, to exceed ( Harris ): see ابهرنا ubharnā.

def: v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
more: Bhoj. v. n. S. उद् away, वस् dwell, वास perfume. 1. To become sticky, slimy, thick, rancid, rank. Kachauryāṅ aisī ubsīṅ, kī tār bandhne lagā . 2. To become mouldy, mildewed, musty (bhuā jānā.) Miṭhāī dharī dharī bus gaī . 3. To be out of patience, wearied, tired, or worn out. Ham to dhare dhare ubas gaë . Wom. (A very expressive idiom). In truth I am worn (or rotted) with lying by.
v. n. To rot, to putrefy.

def: s.m. = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

def: Caus. of ubaṭnā . q. v.
v.t. To cause the body to be rubbed with ubṭan, to have ubṭan rubbed on the body.
s.m. pl. (of ʻābid, q.v.), Holy men, devotees.
s.m. Heat, sultriness, languor occasioned by heat ( = ummas); condolence, sympathy.
n. f. 1. E. Sultry, oppressive heat; langour occasioned by heat. 2. W. Condolence ( sāṅpā ).
more: s. m. Oppressive heat, languor occasioned by heat.

def: s.m. A bear.

ety: S. उद्+भायि, caus. of rt. भी
def: v.t. To alarm, disturb.
more: v. a. To alarm, to disturb.

def: s.m. Rising, swelling, tumefaction; plumpness; prominence; — developing; budding; acquiring fullness; rising or development of the breasts (in girls)
more: n. m. S. उत् + हृ to raise. 1. Swelling; plumpness; prominence. 2. Budding; developing; acquiring fullness. Mazā jo āp ke sīne kī kuchh ubhār meṅ haī, Na seb meṅ, nu bihī meṅ, na woh anār meṅ hai . Inshā. 3. The development or growth of the breasts (in women). 1. Jawānī kā ubhār . 2. Joban kā ubhār .
s. m. Swelling, plumpness, tumefaction, prominence.
subs: (used in compounds), see ubhār lānā, and ubhār le-jānā, s.v. ابهارنا ubhārnā.

def: v. n. 1. To swell; overflow; flood; run over. 2. To exalt, or lift oneself up; to boast; to be a swell (Slang) . Thoṛe pānī meṅ ubhre phirte haiṅ . Prov. E. Little fishes disport in little water. 3. To improve; progress; get on; acquire new strength. 1. Ab to bīmārī se ubhar chale . 2. Diwālā niklā thā, ab phir ubhar chale .
part. (of the next), Taken up, carried away, &c. — ubhārā denā, v.t. To carry off, bear away; to incite, persuade, encourage, entice
subs: v.t. To unload, &c. (see ubhārnā and ubhrānā).

def: v. n. 1. To move; lead; draw over; draw on. 2. To win over; work upon; get to do. 3. To procure; get; obtain.

ety: ubhār˚ = Prk. उब्भारे(इ) or उब्भार(इ) = S. उद्भारय(ति), caus. of rt. उद्+भृ
def: v.t. To raise up, plump up; to take up, lift; to cause to be unladen, to unlade (a cart or boat); to discharge (cargo), unship; to remove, bear away, carry off, run away with; to steal, kidnap (in these senses the more common form is ubhār-le-jānā); to rouse, stir up, incite, excite, instigate, induce, persuade; to raise up, place beyond the reach of danger or harm; to save, rescue, deliver from trouble of any kind), release, liberate, set free. — ubhār-lānā, v.t. To unload and bring; — to incite, induce, work upon, persuade, win over, seduce, draw over; to procure, obtain. — ubhār-le-jānā, v.t. To carry off, bear away, &c. (see above).
more: v. a. 1. To lift; raise up; take up. Gaṭhye ko ubhār de, bhāī . Rus. 2. E. To unfold; open out; unroll; unravel ( udheṛnā ). Khaṭyā ubhār ḍāto . E. 3. E. To cause to be unladen; to unload; unlade; lighten (a boat, etc. ( utārnā ). Naiyā se malwā ubhār lā? Mag. 4. To clear; unship; discharge (cargo). 5. To steal; carry off; smuggle ( ubhārā denā ). Koī merī gaṭhrī ubhār le gayā . 6. To kidnap; run away with; decoy; seduce ( bhagā le jānā). Us ranḍī ko koī ubhār le gayā . 7. To rescue from drowning, poverty, misery, etc. Ḍūbat Brij ubhārā chhin meṅ, Rachchhā to kīnī sab Brij bāl kī . Brij. Rās. Līlā. Brij drowning he uplifted in a trice, Preserved all Brij from being a sacrifice. 8. To free; set at liberty; liberate; save; release. 9. To influence; work upon; prevail upon; win over. Wā ke gawāh kū ubhār liyo . Rus. 10. To persuade; encourage; induce; entice ( ubhārā denā ). Phr. To bait th…
v. a. To plump up, to raise up, to excite, to persuade.

def: v.t. = ابهارنا ubhārnā, q.v. v.t. To raise up, plump up; to take up, lift; to cause to be unladen, to unlade (a cart or boat); to discharge (cargo), unship; to remove, bear away, carry off, run away with; to steal, kidnap (in these senses the more common form is ubhār-le-jānā); to rouse, stir up, incite, excite, instigate, induce, persuade; to raise up, place beyond the reach of danger or harm; to save, rescue, deliver from trouble of any kind), release, liberate, set free. — ubhār-lānā, v.t. To unload and bring; — to incite, induce, work upon, persuade, win over, seduce, draw over; to procure, obtain. — ubhār-le-jānā, v.t. To carry off, bear away, &c. (see above).
more: v. a. To take away, to steal.

ety: S. उद्+भृ; see the trans. form ابهارنا ubhārnā
def: v.n. To rise, swell, be inflated; to come out fully, be developed; to rise up, form (as a pustule); to spring up, shoot, grow, increase, flourish; to run over, overflow; to be or become excited or inflamed, &c.; to feel the sexual passion; to be puffed up, be conceited; to rise from a sick bed; to recover from trouble or misfortune; to rally; to take oneself off; to take leave, depart; to make one's escape, to steal away; to come to light, be discovered or exposed, become public; to be unloaded, unladen, emptied (as a cart or boat). — ubhar ānā = ubharnā in most of its significations. — ubhar ćalnā, To be on the rise or increase; to run over, overflow; to become puffed up, vain, or conceited; to be gaining strength (after sickness, &c.), be convalescent; to be improving; to progress favourably.
more: v. n. H. ūpar up, ā'nā to come. To this class belong, ug'nā, ub'nā , to sprout; upaj'nā , germinate; uṭh'nā , rise; ubas'nā , smell; uplā'nā, utrā'nā , float, emerge; uṛ'nā , fly; uchak'nā, uchaṭ'nā , bound, recoil; uchhal'nā , jump; ukhar'nā, upaṛ'nā , to be uprooted; ulachh'nā, ubachh'nā , to bale; ulaṛ'nā , tilt over; ulaṭ'nā , upset; umaṅd'nā , flow over; ubal'nā , E. usan'nā , boil; auṅṭ'nā to be boiled; uphan'nā , to froth; ugal'nā , spit out; ubak'nā, ok'nā , vomit; ugaṭ'nā , bring up or cast in one's teeth; ukas'nā , stir, move; ujhal'nā , to pour out; ulaṛ'nā, uthal'nā , to be turned topsy-turvy; ughaṛ'nā , to be uncovered; udhaṛ'nā , to be stripped; auṅdh'nā , to be inverted; ubhyā'nā , E. to turn from; uklā'nā , to be restless; udhal'na , to be skittish, wanton. 1. To rise; develop; swell. Kitnī jaldī is kī chhātiyāṅ ubhriṅ! 2. To grow; increase; flourish; spring; shoot; reviv…
(see ابهرانا) v. n. To swell, to rise up, to overflow, to unlade (a cart or boat).

def: = उभय ubhay, q.v.
adj. Both, the two: (in Hindī generally occurring in comp., e.g.) ubhaya-tas, adv. From both sides, on both sides, to both sides; in both cases
subs: adv. In both ways; in both cases
adv. That regards or respects both sides; common to both sides.
ex: Come here right now/at once/this instant.

def: v.n. To rise up, swell; to upheave; to feel a choking sensation (in the throat); to feel weary, tired, or disgusted.
more: v. n. S. भृ to carry, H. ūpar bhar ānā= ubhar ānā . 1. To upheave; swell; to feel one's heart in his throat. 2. To be tired ( uktānā ), disgusted, weary (of life, etc.). 1. Esoṅ jī ubhyā gel he, ki taniko jï na laga le . Mag. Wom. I am so disgusted that I have no heart for any thing. 2. Nahīṅ kehū hit bandhū, kā se kahūṅ pīr, Ubhī ubhī āwe jīarā, bhājata sarīr . Mag. Song. No friendly brother here, to whom relate my pain? My heart comes to my throat, my body from me flies.

def: v.n. To be oppressed with heat, to be faint or languid; to be angry or dissatisfied.
more: Bhoj; v. n. To be faint or languid.
v. n. 1. To be agitated or oppressed with heat. 2. To be angry or dissatisfied.

def: part. adj. Unladen (as a cart or boat); excited, &c.
more: part. Unladen (as a cart or boat).

def: v.t. To cause to overflow, to fill to overflowing, &c. (cf. ubhārnā, of which it is another form).
more: v. a. To fill a vessel till it run over.

def: s.f. Obstacle, impediment, hindrance.
s.m. The slip-knot or noose placed round the neck of a vessel for drawing water with it.
more: W. n. m. The slip-knot round the neck of a vessel for drawing water.
adj. m. Of the present time, now.

def: s.f. The act of vomiting, &c. (see ubaknā).
more: Rus. n. f. The act of vomiting (qaë) .

def: (see ابکنا) s. f. Act of vomiting.
v.t. To cause (one) to vomit; to produce vomiting, &c. (see ubaknā).
v.t. = ابالنا ubālnā, q.v. v.t. To boil, to make boil.
more: (caus. of ابلنا) v. a. To cause to boil (by the hands of another), to boil any thing with such violence that it is spilled.

def: v.n. Lit. 'To be sown or planted'; to germinate; to grow; to be produced; to spring (as plants), sprout, shoot (cf. ugnā).
more: v. n. S. उत up, वज्, गम्, to go (?) To spring; germinate; shoot; sprout; grow.

def: s.m. See ubrā-subrā, s.v. ubarnā. — ubrā-karnā, v.t. = ubrānā, q.v. v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.

E. n. m. 1. Remnants; what is left or not consumed at a meal (bachā bachāyā) . 2. Remains; fragments; leavings; refuse; things left or thrown away; scraps (jhūṭ kūṭ) . Kuchh ubrā subrā bhāt hokhe, to phakīr ke dihīṅ . Mag. Wom. Whatever remains of the rice, give to the beggar.
(causal of ابرنا ubarnā), = ابارنا ubārnā, q.v. v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.
more: v. a. (dakh. caus. of ابرنا ubarnā ) To reserve, keep remaining or in store.

def: n. m. Surface.
v.t. To cause to rot; to cause to ferment; to cause to become sticky.
more: Mag. v. a. To cause to rot or spoil, or become sticky.

ety: S. उद्+वर्तनं, 'rubbing and cleaning the body'
def: s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀯𑁆𑀯𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀡 (uvvaṭṭaṇa), from Sanskrit उद्वर्तन (udvartana).

def: n. m. S. उद्वर्तनं, , Pr. उब्बडणं rubbing or kneading the body. A cosmetic; a composition of gram or barley meal, mixed with turmeric, oil, and other ingredients to soften and clean the skin, and invigorate and beautify the person. This is used especially by the bride and bridegroom for about ten days before the marriage. The ingredients of the composition vary.
s.m. = ابٿن ubṭan
more: v. a. To rub on the body a detergent application, called اپٿن upṭan.
subs: v.t. To anoint and rub the body with ubṭan.

def: s.f. = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

ety: for A. عبودية, v.n. fr. عبد 'to serve'
def: s.f. Service (of God), adoration, devotion ( = ʻibādat); reverence; humble submission, submission, subjection; servitude, &c. ( = ʻabdīyat, q.v.).
more: (from عبد) s. f. Servitude, devotion, subjection, reverence.

def: s.m. Crossing, passing (a river), passing over, traversing; transportation; — going through (a book, &c.), extensive reading or study, proficiency, mastery (of a subject); — a ford, a ferry; a pass
more: n. m. 1. Crossing (a river). 2. Mastery; proficiency. ubūr-i-daryā-i-shor ; H. kālā pānī , Transportation beyond seas. ubūr-i-daryā-i-shor k., kāle pānī bhejnā , v. a. To transport beyond seas. ubūr k. , v. a. To cross (a river).
(from عبر) s. m. Passing, crossing (a river), passing over, a ford, or pass.

def: v.t. To cause to be sown, &c.; to sow (seed).
corr. of اچهاه uććhāh, q.v. s.m. Festival, jubilee, public demonstration of joy; joy, gladness, merry-making
subs: s.m. One who exults; boaster, braggart.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), One who suddenly snatches up a thing and makes off with it; a shop-lifter, pick-pocket, pilferer, thief, knave, swindler, sharper.
s.f. = H اچکاپن उचक्कापन ućakkāpan, s.m. = H اچکاپنا उचक्कापना ućakkāpanā, s.m. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.
s.m. = H اچکاپنا उचक्कापना ućakkāpanā, s.m. = H اچکاگري उचक्कागरी ućakkāgarī, s.f. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.
s.m. = H اچکاپن उचक्कापन ućakkāpan, s.m. = H اچکاگري उचक्कागरी ućakkāgarī, s.f. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.

ety: see ućakkā; and cf. S. वञ्चक
def: v.n. To leap up, bound up, spring up, jump up; to stand on tip-toe; to slip out (of), to drop; to escape, slip away, make off; — v.t. To snatch, snap, pounce upon
subs: ućak le-jānā, v.t. To snatch away; carry off suddenly, or by stealth, or by artifice or fraud; to anticipate (as in utterance or speech).

def: v.n. To be raised or lifted up; to rise; to be jerked up.
v.n. To be separated; to separate (as skin from the flesh, or as scales, scabs, &c.).
more: v. n. To separate, to be separated.

def: v.t. To raise, elevate, exalt; to hoist, to lift up (a burden).
s.f. Taking of goods upon credit; a running account (as with a baniyā); credit; the goods taken on credit; — knavery, knavish tricks, fraud.
more: n. f. S. उ + चप् to grind, cheat. 1. Taking goods on credit; a credit transaction; credit; tick (Slang) . W. 1. Hamārī uske hāṅ uchāpat hai . I have credit there. 2. Banye kī uchāpat, aur ghoṛe kī dauṛ barābar . Prov. A creditor's account goes on running with the speed of a horse. 2. Sharp practice; roguery; fraud. E. Ham-hī se uchāpat lāte ho !

def: s.m. One who gives, or who takes, goods on credit; creditor; debtor; customer; — ućāptī-lekhā, s.m. Account of goods taken on credit, bill.
more: E. n. m. Debtor or creditor, relatively one to the other. Taiṅ to mhārā uchāptī hai, chāëṅ so le jā . (The idiom of Banyās ). uchāp'tī lekhā , n. A running account; a shop bill.

def: adj. Driven away, caused to disappear; banished; scared, terrified; bewildered; wild-looking; separated; alienated, estranged; displeased, offended; dissatisfied; indifferent; weary (of); disgusted (with); disheartened; sad, sorrowful; — s.f. (S. uććāṭana), Driving away; banishing; breaking (as sleep); separation, estrangement, alieṅation; terror, scared look or appearance; longing to be separated; vexation; sadness, sorrow; weariness; disgust
subs: v.n. To be alienated or estranged; to be scared; to feel aversion, weariness, disgust, &c.
v.n. To be scared, terrified, &c.; to be averse (to); disgusted (with); weary (of), &c.
v.n. To be driven away, banished; to be alienated, &c. ( = ućāṭ khānā, q.v.); to be displeased or offended (with -se), be disgusted (with), &c.

def: v.t. To drive or turn away (from), to scare away; to dissuade (from); to banish, to break or disturb (sleep); to separate, divide; to cause to rebound; to cause to splash; to dishearten, discourage; to sadden, to make anxious — ućṭā-denā = ućāṭnā intensified. (See ućṭānā.)
more: (caus. of اچٿنا q. v.) v. a. To divide, separate. اچاٿ هونا uchāṭ honā, To be tired, to be disgusted.

def: v.n. To go away (from, -se); to withdraw (from); to separate, part (from); to divide; to be separated, detached, severed; to come off (as plaster from a wall); to be dropped or lost; to be alienated (from); to be displeased or offended (with); to be weary (of); to be banished, broken, or disturbed (as sleep); to be scared away; to rebound (as a sword striking a hard body obliquely), glance off, recoil, spring back; to spring, bound, leap again; to slip away: — ućaṭ-jānā = ućaṭnā intensified.
s.m. Talking in one's sleep; dreaming uneasily; bawling aloud, or raving, in sleep; incubus or nightmare.
adj. High, lofty, exalted; ascendant, predominant; — s.m. The apex of the orbit of a planet; the sign of the zodiac in which a planet obtains its most powerful influence
more: adj. High; lofty; predominating. uchch kī girah , Lord of the ascendant; good star; good luck. Āj tumārī koī uchch kī girah ā gayī. uchchat , adv. High; aloft. uchgā'rī, uchgarā , adj. S उच्च high, H. गिरह good or evil star. 1. Rightful; lawful; privileged. 2. Wanton; wicked (ironically). uchhāo, ukhāo, uṭhāo , adj. Prominent; projecting; rising; protuberant.
subs: s.m. High rank, station, or office: ućća-pad-dhārī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Holding high office or position; — a person of rank; dignitary; magnate
( = S. grahas), Ascendant planet; good or propitious star.

def: s.f. Height, elevation.
s.m. = اچارن uććāraṇ, q.v. s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
more: s. m. Pronunciation, utterance, articulation, expression.
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
more: n. m. S. उच्चर to utter, उत् up, चर move, A. talaffuz . 1. Orthoepy; pronounciation; utterance; articulation; enunciation. 2. Accentuation; vocalization; accent; delivery; expression. uchchāran kar nā , v. a. 1. To pronounce; utter; articulate; speak up. 2. To express; ejaculate; exclaim; call out.
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: part. Pronounced, uttered, articulated.
= اچارت uććārit, q.v. part. Pronounced, uttered, articulated.
v.t. To pronounce, &c. = uććāraṇ karnā, q.v.s.v. uććāraṇ. s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
more: v. a. To pronounce, speak out, utter, articulate, express.
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: v.n. To rise (as pustules, boils, &c.), to come off, separate (as the skin from the flesh, crust or scales, &c.); — to be pronounced, uttered, spoken, &c.
more: 1. v. n. To separate (as the skin from the flesh, &c.). 2. To be uttered, spoken or pronounced. 3. v. a. To speak, utter, pronounce.

def: adv. On high, aloft.
s.f. Height, loftiness; exaltation.

more: v. n. S. उत् above, शल् to move. 1. To leap; bound; jump; spring. Meṅḍak uchhal rahe haiṅ, aur mor nāchte haiṅ . Barsāt . Nazīr. 2. To gush; spurt; issue; spout. Phuāre se pānī uchhaltā hai . 3. To rejoice; dance and sing; to be merry; to be overjoyed. 1. Āë Kanāgat, phūlā kāṅs, Bāman uchhleṅ nau nau bāṅs . Prov. Kanāgat's come, the long grass blooms, The Brāhman dances nine poles high. (The Kanāgat , or Shrād is a feasting of Brāhmans with oblations made to appease the souls of the dead). 2. Chillā kham ṭhok uchhaltā ho, tab dekh bahāreṅ jāṛe kī. Jāṛā . Nazīr. When Chillā * springs and beats his arms, then is the reign of winter great. 3. Kuchh rāg aur raṅg jhamakate haiṅ, kuchh maë ke jām chhalakte haiṅ, Kuchh kūdeṅ haiṅ, kuchh uchhleṅ haiṅ, kuchh haṅste haiṅ. kuchh bakte haiṅ. Yeh taur, yeh naqshā ashrat kā har ān banāyā Holī ne . def: v.t. To throw up, toss up; to dandle, fling up (a child); to cast up, vomit; to toss about, bandy, treat with disrespect, bring discredit on
more: E. v. a. Caus. of uchhalnā , S. उत + शाल, Pr. uchchhitto tossed up. 1. To toss; toss up; throw; throw up; pitch; fling; wave. 1. Sonā uchhālte chale jāo . Prov. lit . To toss up gold on the road. (A phrase used to express security of life and property). 2. Jo dekhe inkī sūrat. le pyār se khilāve, Hāthoṅ upar uchhāle, aur chheṛ-kar haṅsāve, Chūme kabhī dahan ko, chhātī kabhī lagāve, Koī chusnī muṅh meṅ deve, koī jhunjunā bajāve. Kyā sair dekhte haiṅ yeh tift shīr-khore ! Nazīr. Whoever sees the [dear] face [of a child]. But fondly takes him up, and plays with him, Now throws him up and tickles, makes him laugh, Now kisses him, now clasps him to his breast, One [gently] puts a coral to his mouth, Another turns a rattle [for his sport]. What entertainment springs before a sucking child! 3. Koī to tāne gulchah, koī kīchar Hari pe ulchhāë, Koī saṅg kī sahelī thāṛī mintī kare duār pe. Holī . S…
(caus. of اچهلنا) v. a. To throw up, to toss up a thing (as a ball, &c.) and catch it in the hand.
subs: v.t. (intens. of ućhālnā), To throw or toss up, &c.
